steps.description: Welcome to OpenBowl! OpenBowl is the Universal Context Layer for AI models. This tour will guide you through the core components of our full-stack architecture.
This is the server entrypoint of the Go core sidecar. It loads configurations, seeds the SQLite database, starts file-watching, and boots either the stdio MCP server or the Gin API server.
OpenBowl is local-first. We initialize a WAL-enabled SQLite database holding workspaces, projects, tasks, memories, and message threads.
This is the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server handler. It responds to stdio JSON-RPC 2.0 requests from Cursor or VS Code, exposing project details and memories directly to editor agents.
This is our Chrome extension content script. It runs inside ChatGPT and Claude pages, injecting the 'Inject Context' button and setting up background auto-sync.
Every time the DOM changes (e.g., you write a prompt or the assistant responds), this MutationObserver debounces and sends the chat logs to the Go server backend, preserving context dynamically.
Finally, we maintain high test reliability. This Playwright test suite launches Chromium pre-loaded with the extension, intercepts external domains, and asserts storage and DOM injection behaviors.
